name: Green Mountain
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving six Vermont counties, offering mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and savings products with federally-insured accounts.
description_long:
Green Mountain Credit Union (GMCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in Vermont, serving residents, employees, and members who live, work, or worship in Grand Isle, Franklin, Chittenden, Addison, Rutland, and Washington counties. The institution positions itself with the tagline "Mountains Above the Rest!" and emphasizes personal service and member relationships as core to its mission.

GMCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including mortgages (fixed-rate home purchase and refinancing), auto and truck loans, recreational vehicle financing, personal loans, business loans, energy efficiency loans, credit cards, and savings vehicles (share savings, money market accounts, and share certificates). Their credit card carries a 12.00% fixed APR, while vehicle loans start as low as 5.39% APR for trucks and autos, and 5.89% APR for recreational vehicles. They also offer specialized recreational loans for winter equipment and activities. Share certificates currently offer rates ranging from 3.45% APY (6-month term) to 2.47% APY (24-month term) as of September 2025.

GMCU distinguishes itself through emphasis on personal service, with staff willing to work with members on specialized lending needs (the website explicitly invites members to call about equipment purchases not listed). They highlight 24/7 member support availability, online and mobile banking access, and a team-oriented approach with named loan officers available for consultations. Their membership eligibility is geographically defined and inclusive of anyone in their service counties regardless of employment status.

As a federally-insured credit union (NCUA), GMCU provides account protection up to $250,000 per member, backed by the U.S. government. Member testimonials emphasize responsiveness, friendliness, and smooth loan processes. However, the website provides limited detail on approval rates, underwriting criteria, or comparative rate information, and business loan specifics are vague beyond mentioning "lines of credit, equipment loans, construction loans and real estate mortgages."
services: Fixed-rate mortgages for home purchase and refinancing
Auto and truck loans (rates as low as 5.39% APR)
Recreational vehicle financing (rates as low as 5.89% APR)
Personal loans with predictable fixed payments
Business loans including lines of credit, equipment loans, construction loans, and real estate mortgages
VISA credit cards with 12.00% fixed APR
Specialized recreational equipment loans (skis, snowboards, snow blowers, hockey equipment, vacations)
Share savings accounts and Money Market accounts
Share certificates (CDs) with terms from 6 to 24 months
Online and mobile banking with 24/7 access
pros: Federally-insured accounts (NCUA) protect deposits up to $250,000 backed by U.S. government
Fixed-rate credit card at 12.00% APR eliminates rate-shock concerns
Specialized loan products like energy efficiency financing and winter recreation loans show member-focused innovation
Named loan officers available for personalized service and to discuss non-standard lending requests
Member testimonials specifically praise responsiveness, timeliness, and smooth approval processes
Geographic service area explicitly defined to show community commitment across six Vermont counties
24/7 member support availability for account and banking assistance
cons: Limited membership eligibility—restricted to six Vermont counties (Grand Isle, Franklin, Chittenden, Addison, Rutland, Washington), excluding broader regional or national access
Website lacks transparency on approval rates, underwriting criteria, or debt-to-income requirements for loans
Share certificate rates (2.47–3.50% APY) are modest and no competitive comparison to other credit unions provided
Business loan section is vague with minimal detail on specific terms, rates, or SBA product availability
No information on fees (annual fee, overdraft, ATM usage) or loan origination/prepayment penalties
best_for: Vermont residents and workers in the six-county service area seeking personalized, relationship-based lending
Members prioritizing federal insurance protection and community-focused credit union values over broader branch networks
Homebuyers and refinancers in the region looking for fixed-rate mortgages with local underwriting
Small business owners seeking specialized equipment financing with flexible underwriting from a local institution
diagnosis: Green Mountain Credit Union is best for Vermont residents and business owners in its six-county service area who value personalized, relationship-driven banking and prioritize federal account protection over competitive rate shopping. The main caveat is strict geographic membership restrictions and limited online/national accessibility, making it unsuitable for those outside the service region or seeking broader institutional options.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Green Mountain — NCUA-insured credit union in South Burlingto, VT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
